dependencies: Independent subcontractors perform substantially all construction work
Construction materials readily available from numerous sources
Building materials supply impacted by global supply chain disruptions
Lumber, steel, concrete, resin, copper, and petroleum-based materials influenced by global commodity prices and tariffs
risks: Labor supply shortages and cost of labor
Volatility in construction material costs (lumber, steel, concrete, resin, copper, petroleum-based materials)
Supply chain disruptions impacting building materials availability
Consolidation of ownership of source of supply for certain building materials
Government policies making it more difficult/expensive for suppliers to produce materials (tariffs, import/export restrictions)
Changes in immigration laws affecting labor availability

regulatory_risks: Building codes and land zoning restrictions
Mortgage financing and title operation regulations
FHA and VA mortgage eligibility requirements
Government-sponsored investor rules (Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Ginnie Mae)
Consumer lending rules and disclosure requirements
State and local building moratoria and slow growth initiatives
Climate change and environmental governmental regulation
Upgraded building codes due to climate conditions
Restrictions on land development in certain areas due to climate/flooding risks
Zoning approval requirements in flood-prone areas
Tax law changes affecting deferred tax assets and liabilities
Section 382 ownership change limitations on NOL utilization
Fraudulent conveyance laws affecting guarantor subsidiaries

sanction_risks: Imposition of tariffs (in particular on materials imported from Canada or Mexico)
Import or export restrictions, penalties or sanctions
Modification or elimination of international agreements covering trade or investment
